title: Fix race condition in memcached-pod-kill chaos test
summary: Replaces the racey Pod-level kubectl wait (Ready=false then Ready=true) in step 4 of the memcached-pod-kill chaos test with Deployment-level readyReplicas polling, following the established pattern from operator-pod-crash and api-pod-kill-pdb. The script polls openstack-memcached Deployment readyReplicas in two phases: Phase 1 confirms chaos took effect (readyReplicas < 3), Phase 2 confirms recovery (readyReplicas == 3). All 6 Keystone condition assertions are preserved verbatim. The NOTE block about the theoretical race is replaced with a CC-0076 reference explaining the Deployment-level approach. Documentation in docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md accurately reflects the new step-4 type and design rationale. Only 2 files modified (test + docs), matching the planned scope exactly.

review_process.step_1_intent: Fix race condition where kubectl wait resolves label selector once, then watches the specific Pod — when Chaos Mesh deletes it, the watch errors with NotFound instead of re-resolving onto the replacement Pod.
review_process.step_2_scope: Only 2 source files changed: tests/e2e-chaos/memcached-pod-kill/chainsaw-test.yaml and docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md. No scope creep. Progress JSON is expected infrastructure.
review_process.step_3_verification: YAML/shell test — no pytest/mypy/ruff applicable. Manual structural review performed against reference implementations.
review_process.step_4_patterns: Script follows the operator-pod-crash Deployment polling pattern exactly: no set -euo pipefail, ${READY:-0} defensive defaults, Phase 1 + Phase 2 structure, explicit error messages with exit 1.
review_process.step_5_tests: This IS the test — a Chainsaw E2E chaos test. Verified assert block preserved verbatim (diff confirmed zero differences).
review_process.step_6_security: No security concerns — test infrastructure running in ephemeral CI cluster.
review_process.step_7_complexity: Simplest viable solution — reuses established Deployment polling pattern verbatim from operator-pod-crash.
review_process.step_8_completeness: Both planned tasks implemented: Task 1.1 (replace wait with Deployment polling) and Task 1.2 (update docs). All 6 acceptance criteria met.

acceptance_criteria_verification.AC1_no_wait_with_ready_false: PASS — Both wait sub-steps (Ready=false and Ready=true on kind: Pod) removed from step 4
acceptance_criteria_verification.AC2_resilient_mechanism: PASS — Deployment readyReplicas polling is resilient to pod replacement; watches the persistent Deployment object, not individual Pods
acceptance_criteria_verification.AC3_note_block_removed: PASS — NOTE block (lines 50-56 on main) removed; step-4 comment references CC-0076 and issue #214
acceptance_criteria_verification.AC4_conditions_preserved: PASS — All 6 assertions preserved verbatim: SecretsReady, FernetKeysReady, DatabaseReady, DeploymentReady, BootstrapReady, Ready (AllReady)
acceptance_criteria_verification.AC5_ci_runs: DEFERRED — Requires 3 consecutive CI runs; structural correctness verified via pattern comparison
acceptance_criteria_verification.AC6_no_memcached_operator_changes: PASS — No files outside forge repo modified

tests_checklist.item: Test exists (this IS the test being fixed)
Explicit failure on Chaos Mesh misconfiguration (exit 1 if readyReplicas never drops)
All 6 Keystone condition assertions preserved verbatim

code_quality_checklist.item: set -euo pipefail intentionally omitted with documented rationale
Clear Phase 1 / Phase 2 structure with echo status messages
Step-4 comment references CC-0076 and issue #214

security_checklist.item: No shell injection vectors (Deployment name and namespace are controlled)

architecture_checklist.item: Solution reuses established Deployment polling pattern (3rd instance)
Scope limited to test file + docs (no operator changes)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code (script follows existing pattern, not copy-paste of unrelated logic)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Phase 1 exits 1 if readyReplicas never drops (Chaos Mesh misconfiguration detection)
Phase 2 exits 1 if memcached never recovers

defensive_checklist.item: ${READY:-0} prevents failure on empty kubectl output
Script timeout (120s) provides hard upper bound

suggested_improvements: The mandatory pattern file .planwerk/patterns/chaos-e2e-test-suite-structure-with-degradationrecovery-and-no-regression-patter.md still describes 'a 30s sleep before assertion' for the no-regression pattern, which has been stale since CC-0047 (replaced with condition-based waits) and now further evolved to Deployment-level polling. Consider updating the pattern description to reflect the current approach. This predates CC-0076 and is not a regression from this change.
next_steps: Run 3 consecutive CI e2e-chaos jobs to confirm deterministic pass (AC5)

detected_patterns.name: Deployment readyReplicas polling for chaos effect confirmation
detected_patterns.applies_when: Adding a chaos E2E test that needs to confirm pod disruption took effect, especially when the target pod may be deleted and replaced by its controller (ReplicaSet/Deployment) before kubectl wait can observe the transition
detected_patterns.description: Instead of using chainsaw wait with kind: Pod and a label selector (which resolves the selector once and watches the specific Pod object — failing with NotFound when the Pod is deleted), poll the parent Deployment's status.readyReplicas via kubectl get with jsonpath. The script has two phases: Phase 1 polls until readyReplicas drops below the desired count (chaos confirmed), Phase 2 polls until readyReplicas returns to the desired count (recovery confirmed). Uses ${READY:-0} defensive defaults for empty kubectl output. Omits set -euo pipefail because the defensive defaults would fail under set -e. Each phase has a bounded for-loop with sleep 2 and an explicit exit 1 on timeout with a descriptive error message. The script step has a timeout (120-150s) as a hard upper bound.

examples.code:
- script:
        timeout: 150s
        content: |
          # NOTE: set -euo pipefail is intentionally omitted. The polling loop
          # uses ${READY:-0} defensive defaults that would fail under set -e
          # when kubectl returns empty output, and the loop's exit condition
          # is checked explicitly rather than relying on exit codes.
          # Phase 1: poll until readyReplicas drops below 2 (chaos took effect).
          for i in $(seq 1 60); do
            READY=$(kubectl get deployment keystone-operator -n default -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}')
            if [ "${READY:-0}" -lt 2 ]; then
              break
            fi
            sleep 2
          done
          if [ "${READY:-0}" -ge 2 ]; then
            echo "ERROR: readyReplicas never dropped below 2 — pod kill did not take effect"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "Pod kill confirmed: readyReplicas=${READY:-0}"
          # Phase 2: poll until readyReplicas returns to 2 (operator recovered).
          for i in $(seq 1 60); do
            READY=$(kubectl get deployment keystone-operator -n default -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}')
            if [ "${READY:-0}" -eq 2 ]; then
              echo "Operator recovered: readyReplicas=2"
              exit 0
            fi
            sleep 2
          done
          echo "ERROR: operator did not recover — readyReplicas=${READY:-0}"
          exit 1

- script:
        timeout: 120s
        content: |
          # NOTE: set -euo pipefail is intentionally omitted. The polling loop
          # uses ${READY:-0} defensive defaults that would fail under set -e
          # when kubectl returns empty output, and the loop's exit condition
          # is checked explicitly rather than relying on exit codes.
          # Phase 1: poll until readyReplicas drops below 3 (chaos took effect).
          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
            READY=$(kubectl get deployment openstack-memcached -n $NAMESPACE -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}')
            if [ "${READY:-0}" -lt 3 ]; then
              break
            fi
            sleep 2
          done
          if [ "${READY:-0}" -ge 3 ]; then
            echo "ERROR: readyReplicas never dropped below 3 — pod kill did not take effect"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "Pod kill confirmed: readyReplicas=${READY:-0}"
          # Phase 2: poll until readyReplicas returns to 3 (memcached recovered).
          for i in $(seq 1 60); do
            READY=$(kubectl get deployment openstack-memcached -n $NAMESPACE -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}')
            if [ "${READY:-0}" -eq 3 ]; then
              echo "Memcached recovered: readyReplicas=3"
              exit 0
            fi
            sleep 2
          done
          echo "ERROR: memcached did not recover — readyReplicas=${READY:-0}"
          exit 1

- script:
        timeout: 120s
        content: |
          # NOTE: set -euo pipefail is intentionally omitted. The polling loop
          # uses ${READY:-0} defensive defaults that would fail under set -e
          # when kubectl returns empty output, and the loop's exit condition
          # is checked explicitly rather than relying on exit codes.
          # Poll until readyReplicas < 3 (confirms pod kill took effect)
          for i in $(seq 1 60); do
            READY=$(kubectl get deployment keystone-chaos-api-api -n $NAMESPACE -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}')
            if [ "${READY:-0}" -lt 3 ]; then
              break
            fi
            sleep 2
          done
          # Fail explicitly if the kill never took effect
          if [ "${READY:-0}" -ge 3 ]; then
            echo "ERROR: readyReplicas never dropped below 3 — pod kill did not take effect"
            exit 1
          fi
